headline: OpenAI confidentially files for IPO, targeting up to $1T valuation
summary: OpenAI filed a confidential S-1 with the SEC on May 22, 2026, targeting a Q4 2026 listing at an $852B-$1T valuation and aiming to raise about $60B, with Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley leading. The company is at a ~$25B annualized revenue run rate but burned ~$9B in 2025 and estimates it needs ~$207B more capital through 2030 to honor compute commitments. A landmark validation of the AI capital cycle that underpins demand for GPUs, datacenters, and power.

headline: Asia chip rally extends: KOSPI tops 8,100, SK Hynix breaks 2M won, Nikkei clears 65,000
summary: Korea's KOSPI surpassed 8,100 for the first time on May 26, 2026 as SK Hynix broke above 2,000,000 won (+6.85%) and Samsung Electronics hit 301,000 won (+2.91%); the two now make up a record ~42% of the index. Japan's Nikkei 225 closed above 65,000 for the first time (+2.87% May 25) and Taiwan's Taiex rose 3.26%. Drivers: easing Middle East tensions (a mooted US-Iran ceasefire and Strait of Hormuz reopening sent oil ~7% lower), foreign buyers returning after 13 sessions of selling, and continued AI memory demand. Nomura raised its KOSPI target to 11,000.

headline: Big earnings week for tracked names: Salesforce, Snowflake, Dell report
summary: A heavy AI-infrastructure earnings week kicks off after the Memorial Day holiday. Salesforce (CRM) and Snowflake (SNOW) report after the close Wednesday, May 27, and Dell Technologies (DELL) reports after the close Thursday, May 28. Focus will be on the durability of enterprise AI software spend (CRM, SNOW) and AI server demand and margins (DELL). Marvell also reports May 27.

headline: Oracle reworks New Mexico AI campus to fuel-cell microgrid, bypassing the grid
summary: Oracle is rethinking on-site power at its planned Project Jupiter campus in New Mexico, replacing gas turbines and diesel backup with a fuel-cell-based microgrid to support high-density AI workloads. The move underscores hyperscalers turning to behind-the-meter generation to skip multi-year grid interconnection queues, a tailwind for fuel-cell suppliers such as Bloom Energy (BE).

headline: Microsoft to acquire ~3,200 acres in Cheyenne, Wyoming for datacenter expansion
summary: Microsoft plans to acquire roughly 3,200 acres in Cheyenne, Wyoming, expanding its long-standing datacenter footprint in the region as it races to add AI compute capacity. The land bank adds to the broader hyperscaler buildout that is straining power and equipment supply chains.
